content:
It’s time to pull it back on OTUS ("_ Of the United States") acronyms. We’ve simply gone too far.
POTUS - I’m okay with this. Has a faux-Latin feel to it, and western politicians do like to cosplay Roman Republic.
FLOTUS - Does have an elegant, floral ring to it. When we have a First Gentleman it will become dangerously close to a homophobic slur.
SCOTUS - Sounds like a venereal disease.
VPOTUS - This is just obscene, completely aura-less. Somehow worse than “Veep.”
BRUTUS - This is not an acronym, this is the Roman Senator that betrayed Julius Caesar.
COTUS - This one stands for the Constitution of the United States. Obvious coitus joke aside, this is the syndrome that results from untreated SCOTUS.
SLOTUS - You’re just messing with me now.
title: OTUS Acronyms are out of hand
Why I always order a Tom Collins

title: Contact Me

content:
Vinny Seeram is a stand-up comedian based in New York City. His draws from his experiences growing up as a second generation Indo-Guyanese immigrant in the Bronx, and his observations on politics, race, and culture.
Vinny performs all over New York, most frequently at his home club Q.E.D. Astoria, where he hosts a weekly open mic and his comedy showcase show, vinnydotcomedy. He has also been featured in festivals across the United States, including:
Boston Comedy Festival (Boston, MA) Vice City Comedy Festival (Miami, FL) Mountain Fresh Comedy Festival (Colorado) Crooked Mouth Comedy Festival (Endicott, NY) Comedy Mob Festival (New York, NY), Semi-finalist HaHa Harvest Comedy Festival (Jersey City, NJ) Salem Comedy and Spirits Festival (Salem, MA) Last Word Comedy Festival (Williamsburg, VA) Vinny has also appeared as an actor in several theatrical productions in New York. In AfterWork Theater’s productions of You Can’t Take it With You and The Crucible he played Tony Kirby and John Proctor, respectively. He has most recently appeared in ScriptClub NYC’s production of The 39 Steps as The Professor and other supporting roles.
title: About
